full_text:
TLS project meeting

12-12-2025

tCycIF - progress, main questions and future plans

Progress Update
Sample Processing Complete: All samples have been subjected to QC and processed.
Data Refinement:
Improved filtering and normalization applied.
Gating performed for all channels.
Phenotyping Approaches Explored:
Manual gating
TRIBUS analysis
Tissue Domain Detection Optimized: UTAG method employed for optimization.

Main questions
Which is the heterogeneity in iTLS/mTLS abundance and distribution? <- Explore clinical associations.
Can we find distinct spatial conformations of TLS? How do they correlate with GeoMx data and trajectory? And with the BCR/iCAN data?
How do iTLS/mTLS presence and abundance correlate with other tissue domains (e.g., immune hubs) and immune infiltration?

Next 3-month Plans
Phenotyping: Complete TRIBUS analysis and explore the use of CellTune.
Obtain UTAG Domains: to gather more information of the tumor architecture
TLS Hub Annotation: Annotate the identified TLS hubs using the Spatial cells package//spacestat?
Explore TLS Composition: Investigate the cellular composition across the identified TLS hubs.
Classify TLSs: Categorize the identified TLSs

H&E - progress, main questions and future plans

Progress Update
All available H&Es checked for TLS → 449 tumors/346 patients 
Clinical data obtained 
Histolytics cell segmentation code modified, segmentation started 
Giotto package tested for defining TLS (cell number/proximity) 
7 potentially milky spot positive samples → 4-plex CycIF planned

Main questions
What is the TLS profile in ovarian cancer (number, maturation stage, distribution within TME)? 
How TLS profile is associated with clinical and pathological factors? 
Can we reliably distinguish TLS maturation stages visually from H&Es? 
Can we detect milky spots from benign/borderline tumors and differentiate them from Lagg?

Next 3-month Plans
Finalize Histolytics segmentation (12/25) 
Continuing clinical analyses for already existing TLS data (12/25-1/26) 
Optimize metrics for TLS with Giotto (12/25-1/26) 
Downstream analyses after Giotto (Ziqi, Elias, Alexandra) (1-2/26) 
Milky spot tCycIF staining+analysis (1-3/26)

GeoMx - progress, main questions and future plans

Progress Update
Quality issue is checked and controlled
GeoMx data are filtered with only TLS ROIs and then QCed and normalized. UMAPs to check distribution
Series of R scripts are ready for GeoMx downstream analysis
ROI re-annotation (new TLS label) is done based on GC signature scores and verified by heatmaps
Pseudotime analysis by SCORPIUS showed TLS maturation trajectory
DEGs and GSEA are explored
Batch3 GeoMx ROIs are aligned and transferred to adjacent tcycif images
MiXCR BCR profiling on iCAN Bulk RNAseq data

Main questions
Defining TLS Maturation Trajectories (LAgg → iTLS → mTLS) and validating pseudo-time inference - real?
What are the molecular dynamics of TLS structural formation and functional maturation? - sequential molecular programs and temporal dynamics
Are TLS maturation levels consistent within a single patient, or do multiple maturation states coexist?
How does TLS maturation relate to the composition and spatial organization of the surrounding TME?

Next 3-month Plans
Further explore interesting TLS related pathways, key genes / related functions
Check chemokine and cytokines network in TLS region
Ligand-receptor analysis with BulksignalR + CellChat + NicheNet to explore cell-cell interactions
Deconvolution analysis for TLS cell fractions distributions

network in TLS region
Ligand-receptor analysis with BulksignalR + CellChat + NicheNet to explore cell-cell interactions
Deconvolution analysis for TLS cell fractions distributions
Spatial analysis on intra-TLS regions, between mature/immature TLSs and check single patient TLSs heterogeneity 
tCycIF analysis on aligned GeoMx ROIs
BCR profiling results interpretation

Scellex pilot plan

Samples for SCellex:
one sample with FF and FFPE (S120): high amount of TLS/B cells
FFPE 1 scroll + 5 scrolls > two tubes
FF sample > how to take it? > one tube
one sample with some TLS/B cells > what sample?

 Questions:
FF sample handling and taking to SCellex
is it free for us?
the thickness for one scrape
